title: Manager, Wealth Management Operations
description:
In this role, you will support the project management team and be the contact point to implement the Wealth Management (WM) back-office system in Singapore, coordinating system setup, testing, and integration across the different teams involved. Once the system is operational, you will manage daily settlement operations for WM products, including trade settlements, payment instructions, and reconciliation.


Key Responsibilities:

  • Assist the project management team in gathering system requirements from the relevant stakeholders, coordinating system implementation and conducting system testing.
  • Assist in reviewing the system’s functionality with the Product, IT, Compliance and other teams involved.
  • Prepare client data structure for integration, either by transforming the existing data or storing the relevant client data in a compatible format.
  • Gather the coverage and booking model of the SG CorpAct Module for analysis.
  • Establish the relevant SOPs and ensure operational readiness.
  • Create standard document templates, including statements and confirmations.
  • Manage daily settlement operations for WM products, including trade settlements, payment instructions, and reconciliation, once the system is live.
  • Any other ad-hoc duties assigned.


Requirements:

  • 5 years of relevant experience in Wealth Management operations, and experience in implementation and UAT of a new system.
  • Familiarity with Wealth Management business and the relevant products.
  • Bachelor’s degree in Banking/ Finance/ Business Management or related fields.
  • Ability to speak/read Chinese to liaise with mandarin-speaking clients and review relevant documents in Chinese.
  • Internal control and internal audit mindset.
  • Detail-oriented and strong communication skills.
  • Ability to multitask and work independently.
  • Ability to work in a fast-paced environment.

skills.skill: Work in a Fast Paced Environment

postedCompany.description:
Effective 2nd October 2017, KGI Securities Co. Ltd. (Taiwan)’s operations in Singapore, namely KGI Securities (Singapore) Pte. Ltd. and KGI Futures (Singapore) Pte. Ltd. are amalgamated into a single entity with KGI Securities (Singapore) Pte. Ltd. as the operating entity. This amalgamation is an internal reorganization exercise. By operation of law, all the property, rights, privileges, liabilities and obligations of KGI Futures (Singapore) Pte. Ltd. will be automatically transferred to and vest in the amalgamated KGI Securities (Singapore) Pte. Ltd.


The amalgamation of our Singapore entities taps on the cooperative synergies of both companies and offer a one-stop service to our clients in Asia. The amalgamated KGI Securities (Singapore) Pte. Ltd. will no longer be just a single dimension equity brokerage house. Instead, it will evolve into a multi products and client centric solutions entity. Here we aim to serve Individuals, Financial Institutions & Corporates in the Region. The products and services we offer include dealing in securities (online and mobile trading), margin financing (local and foreign securities), futures, commodities, options, contract for differences, leveraged FX trading and fixed income. Furthermore, we also provide research services as well as custody services for securities trading.


We are a pioneer Securities & Derivatives, Trading and Clearing Member of Singapore Exchange as well as a pioneer member of ICE Futures Singapore & ICE Clear Singapore. We offer our valued clients round the clock (24 hour) single-touch point for multi-asset clients and 24 hour high-touch desk for global products. We are supported by our strong parentage - KGI Securities Co. Ltd., which is the second largest securities firm in Taiwan in terms of market share of the brokerage business and a wholly owned securities arm of TWSE-listed China Development Financial Holding Corporation.


KGI Securities (Singapore) Pte. Ltd. which is a holder of a Capital Markets Services Licence regulated by the Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) has expanded its regulated activities to include Leverage Foreign Exchange Trading. It is a member of SGX-ST Securities Trading, SGX-DT Derivatives Trading, SGX-DC Derivatives Clearing and CDP Securities Clearing. It is also a Member of ICE Futures Singapore and ICE Clear Singapore and an Associate Member of the Rubber Market of Tokyo Commodity Exchange.


KGI Securities (Singapore) is now a Pioneer Clearing Member of Asia Pacific Exchange (APEX), Singapore’s Third Derivatives Exchange.
postedCompany.name: KGI SECURITIES (SINGAPORE) PTE. LTD.

districts.location: D01 Cecil, Marina, People’s Park, Raffles Place
